what_it_does: Defines a schema fragment for recording what was annotated: a target URI, a W3C TextQuoteSelector anchor, and an immutable snapshot reference of the content as seen by the annotator.
observations: YAML frontmatter marks this as a current block-type noticeboard item sourced from claude_code
The title and top heading are placeholders reading '--- the target (what was annotated) ---'
Body lists three fields: target, target_quote, and captured_at
target_quote is specified as a W3C TextQuoteSelector with exact, prefix, and suffix
captured_at is a cas://<hash> labeled IMMUTABLE and tied to 'what he actually annotated'
The item links via part_of to board://item-e42d651f
created and updated timestamps are identical
standouts: Explicit immutability requirement on the captured_at snapshot
Use of W3C TextQuoteSelector as a re-attach anchor
concerns: Title and heading are placeholders, not descriptive names
No definition of how the cas:// hash is computed
target field syntax is illustrative rather than a formal schema
notes: A noticeboard schema stub that records the annotated node, its exact textual anchor, and an immutable snapshot of the content the annotator saw.
questions: What does 'P8' in the title signify?
How is the cas:// hash generated and verified?
Is target_quote required for every annotation or only for text targets?
What is the relationship to the parent item board://item-e42d651f?
What are the valid values for ui://<kind>?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of defining the annotation target and its captured snapshot matches the content, though the title and heading remain placeholder text.
apparent_themes: Provenance and immutability of annotated content
URI-based addressing for live and snapshot references
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a stable, reproducible way to identify and snapshot the object of an annotation so annotations can be re-anchored even if the live node changes.
novelty: Explicit separation between the live target URI, a W3C TextQuoteSelector re-attachment anchor, and an immutable content snapshot reference.
contribution: Provides a concrete field-level schema for annotation targets and a clear provenance snapshot reference, which a fused system could keep as a pattern for anchoring annotations to mutable content.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item defining the target section of an annotation schema with target URI, W3C TextQuoteSelector, and immutable captured snapshot reference.
intention_for_embedding: To standardize how the annotated object, its exact textual anchor, and the immutable viewed content are recorded.
